values: This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that the transaction is signed by one of the ten specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, ..., `key_10`).
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that the transaction is signed by one of the eight specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, ..., `key_8`).

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that the transaction is signed by one of the four specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`, `key_4`).
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that the transaction is signed by one of the three specified public keys (`holder_1`, `holder_2`, `holder_3`).
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that the transaction is signed by one of the five members (member_1, member_2, member_3, member_4, member_5).
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that the transaction is signed by one of the four members (member_1, member_2, member_3, member_4).
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that the transaction is signed by one of the three specified public keys (`parent_1_key`, `parent_2_key`, `child_key`).
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that the transaction is signed by one of the three partners (`partner_1`, `partner_2`, `partner_3`).

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that the transaction is signed by one of the five specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`, `key_4`, `key_5`).
and(hash160(H), thresh(1, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3), pk(key_4)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. One of the four specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`, `key_4`) signs.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 followed by RIPEMD-160) is provided.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. One of the three specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`) signs.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 followed by RIPEMD-160) is provided.
and(hash256(H), thresh(1, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3), pk(key_4), pk(key_5)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. One of the five specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`, `key_4`, `key_5`) signs.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 applied twice) is provided.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. One of the three specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`) signs.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 applied twice) is provided.

2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 applied twice) is provided.

1. One of the ten specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, ..., `key_10`) signs.

1. Two of the six specified public keys (`friend_key_1`, `friend_key_2`, ..., `friend_key_10`) sign.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 applied twice) is provided.
and(older(57600), thresh(6, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3), pk(key_4), pk(key_5), pk(key_6), pk(key_7), pk(key_8), pk(key_9), pk(key_10), pk(key_11)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. Six of the eleven specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, ..., `key_11`) sign.
2. The nSequence value is at least 57600 blocks (approximately 400 days old).

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. Public key `key_1` signs.
2. Public key `key_2` signs or the nSequence value is at least 144 blocks (approximately 1 day old)

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. Public key `key_user` signs.
2. Public key `key_service` signs or the nSequence value is at least 12960 blocks (approximately 90 days old) - with the first path being 99 times more likely to be used than the second path.
and(ripemd160(H), thresh(1, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3), pk(key_4), pk(key_5), pk(key_6), pk(key_7), pk(key_8), pk(key_9)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. One of the nine specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, ..., `key_9`) signs.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using RIPEMD-160) is provided.
and(ripemd160(H), thresh(1, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3), pk(key_4), pk(key_5)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. One of the five specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`, `key_4`, `key_5`) signs.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using RIPEMD-160) is provided.

1. One of the ten specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, ..., `key_10`) signs.

1. One of the three specified public keys (`prog_key_1`, `prog_key_2`, `prog_key_3`) signs.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256) is provided.
and(sha256(H), thresh(1, pk(team_key_1), pk(team_key_2), pk(team_key_3), pk(team_key_4), pk(team_key_5)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. One of the five specified public keys (`team_key_1`, `team_key_2`, `team_key_3`, `team_key_4`, `team_key_5`) signs.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256) is provided.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. Three of the ten specified public keys (`compet_key_1`, `compet_key_2`, ..., `compet_key_10`) sign.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256) is provided.

1. Two of the three specified public keys (`assist_1`, `assist_2`, `assist_3`) sign.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 applied twice) is provided.
and(thresh(2, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3), pk(key_4), pk(key_5), pk(key_6), pk(key_7)), ripemd160(H))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. Two of the seven specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, ..., `key_7`) sign.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using RIPEMD-160) is provided.
and(thresh(2, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3), pk(key_4)), ripemd160(H))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. Two of the four specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`, `key_4`) sign.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using RIPEMD-160) is provided.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that two of the following conditions are met:
1. Two of the three specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`) sign.
4. The nLockTime value is at least 823937 (block height).

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. Two of the three specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`) sign.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 followed by RIPEMD-160) is provided.
and(thresh(2, pk(op_1), pk(op_2), pk(op_3), pk(op_4)), hash256(H))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. Two of the four specified public keys (`op_1`, `op_2`, `op_3`, `op_4`) sign.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 applied twice) is provided.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that both of the following conditions are met:
1. Two of the three specified public keys (`op_1`, `op_2`, `op_3`) sign.

1. Two of the three specified public keys (`senior_1`, `senior_2`, `senior_3`) sign.
2. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256) is provided.
and(thresh(3, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3), pk(key_4), pk(key_5)), after(869245))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that two of the following conditions are met:
1. Three of the five specified public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`, `key_4`, `key_5`) sign.
4. The nLockTime value is at least 869245 (block height).

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. Public key `key_organization` signs
2. Both the public key named `key_researcher` signs and a waiting period of 52560 blocks (approximately 1 year) has passed since the transaction was included in a block.
The second path is 99 times more likely to be used than the first.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. Public key `key_1` signs and the nSequence value is at least 11520 blocks (approximately 80 days).
2. Either `key_2` or `key_3` signs the transaction.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. Public key `C` signs
2. Both public keys `A` and `B` sign

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. The father signs
2. Both the caregiver and one of the two sons sign
or(and(pk(director),thresh(1, pk(trader_1), pk(trader_2), pk(trader_3), pk(trader_4))), pk(ceo))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. The CEO signs
2. Both the director and one of the four traders sign

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. The owner signs
2. Both the manager and one of the three ops sign

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. Public key `eldest_child_key` signs
2. Both public keys `parent1_key` and `parent2_key` sign

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. Public key `key_company` signs
2. Both the public key named `key_employee` signs and a waiting period of 25920 blocks (approximately 180 days) has passed since the transaction was included in a block.
The first path is 99 times more likely to be used than the second.
or(and(pk(treasurer),thresh(3, pk(member_1), pk(member_2), pk(member_3), pk(member_4), pk(member_5))), pk(president))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. The president signs
2. Both the treasurer and one of the five members sign

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. Public key `key_revocation` signs
2. Both the public key named `key_local` signs and a waiting period of 1008 blocks (approximately 7 days) has passed since the transaction was included in a block.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy specifies that funds can be spent in one of two ways:
1. Public key `KeyA` signs
2. Both the public key named `KeyB` signs and a waiting period of 144 blocks (approximately 1 day) has passed since the transaction was included in a block.
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that public key `key_1` signs the transaction and that a secret preimage that hashes to value `HASH` (using SHA-256 followed by RIPEMD-160) is provided.

1. Public key `key_1` signs.
2. Public key `key_2` signs.
3. Public key `key_3` signs.
4. The nSequence value is at least 2880 blocks (approximately 20 days old).

1. Public key `key_1` signs.
2. Public key `key_2` signs.
3. Public key `key_3` signs.
4. A secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256) is provided.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that all of the following conditions are met:
1. Public key `key1` signs.
2. Public key `key2` signs.
3. Public key `key3` signs.
4. The nLockTime value is at least 1670047200, which corresponds to December 3 2022 at 06:00:00 UTC.
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that all four specified public keys (`key1`, `key2`, `key3`, `key4`) sign.
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that all five specified public keys (`key1`, `key2`, `key3`, `key4`, `key5`) sign.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that all of the following conditions are met:
1. Public key `A` signs.
2. Public key `B` signs.
3. Public key `C` signs.
4. Public key `D` signs.
5. Public key `E` signs.
6. The nLockTime value is at least 1893456000, which corresponds to January 1 2030 at 00:00:00 UTC.
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that all seven specified public keys (`key1`, `key2`, ..., `key7`) sign.
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that all six specified public keys (key1, key2, ..., key6) sign.
This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that all nine members (`member_1`, `member_2`, ..., `member_9`) sign.
or(2@thresh(2, pk(op_1), pk(op_2), pk(op_3)), thresh(1, pk(partner_1), pk(partner_2)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Two out of three public keys (`op_1`, `op_2`, `op_3`) sign.
2. One out of two partners (`partner_1`, `partner_2`) sign.
The policy indicates that the first condition is twice as likely to be used than the second condition.
or(5@thresh(3, pk(member_1), pk(member_2), pk(member_3), pk(member_4)), thresh(1, pk(chair_1), pk(chair_2)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Three out of four members sign.
2. One out of two chairs sign (`chair_1`, `chair_2`).
The policy indicates that the first condition is five times more likely to be used than the second condition.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `key_likely` signs.
2. Public key `key_unlikely` signs.
The policy indicates that the first condition is nine times more likely to be used than the second condition.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `eldest_child_key` signs.
2. Either `parent1_key` or `parent2_key` signs.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `emergencyKey` signs.
2. Public key `unvaultKey` signs after a delay of at least 1008 blocks (approximately 7 days).

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `key_1` signs.
2. Either `key_2` or `key_3` signs.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `key_1` signs.
2. Public key `key_2` signs.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `key_revocation` signs.
2. Public key `key_remote` signs and either public key `key_local` signs and a secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 followed by RIPEMD-160) is provided, or a waiting period of 1008 blocks (approximately 7 days) has passed since the transaction was included in a block.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `key_revocation` signs.
2. Public key `key_remote` signs and either public key `key_local` signs or a secret preimage that hashes to value `H` (using SHA-256 followed by RIPEMD-160) is provided.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `key_unlikely` signs.
2. Public key `key_likely` signs.
The policy indicates that the second condition is 99 times more likely to be used than the first condition.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `KeyA` signs.
2. Public key `KeyB` signs and `KeyB` after a delay of at least 144 blocks (approximately 1 day).
or(thresh(1, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3)), thresh(2, pk(key_4), pk(key_5)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. One out of three public keys `key_1`, `key_2` and `key_3` signs.
2. Both public keys `key_4` and `key_5` sign.
or(thresh(2, pk(op_1), pk(op_2), pk(op_3), pk(op_4), pk(op_5)), thresh(1, pk(partner_1), pk(partner_2)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Two out of five public keys (`op_1`, `op_2`, `op_3`, `op_4`, `op_5`) sign.
2. One out of two partners (`partner_1`, `partner_2`) sign.
or(thresh(2, pk(op_1), pk(op_2), pk(op_3)), 2@thresh(1, pk(partner_1), pk(partner_2)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Two out of three public keys (`op_1`, `op_2`, `op_3`) sign.
2. One out of two partners (`partner_1`, `partner_2`) sign.
The policy indicates that the second condition is twice as likely to be used than the first condition.
or(thresh(2, pk(op_1), pk(op_2), pk(op_3)), 3@thresh(1, pk(partner_1), pk(partner_2)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Two out of three public keys (`op_1`, `op_2`, `op_3`) sign.
2. One out of two partners (`partner_1`, `partner_2`) sign.
The policy indicates that the second condition is three times more likely to be used than the first condition.
or(thresh(3, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3)), thresh(2, pk(key_4), pk(key_5)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Two out of three public keys (`key_1`, `key_2`, `key_3`) sign.
2. Both public keys `key_4` and `key_5` sign.
or(thresh(3, pk(sibling_1), pk(sibling_2), pk(sibling_3), pk(sibling_4), pk(sibling_5)), thresh(1, pk(father), pk(mother)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Three out of five siblings sign.
2. Either the father or mother signs.

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Either `key_2` or `key_3` signs.
2. `key_1` signs and the transaction has an nSquence value that is at least 12096 blocks (approximately 84 days old).

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. Public key `key_1` signs.
2. Public key `key_2` signs.
or(thresh(3, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3)), thresh(2, pk(key_4), pk(key_5)))

This Bitcoin miniscript policy requires that one of the two following conditions is met:
1. `thresh(3, pk(key_1), pk(key_2), pk(key_3))`: this condition is met if all three keys `key_1`, `key_2`, and `key_3` sign.
2. `thresh(2, pk(key_4), pk(key_5))`: this condition is met if both `key_4` and `key_5` sign.

The `after(NUM)` policy term specifies that the transaction can only be included in a block or considered valid after a certain point in time. If `NUM` is less than 500 million, it represents a block height, meaning the transaction can only be included after the blockchain reaches that height; if `NUM` is 500 million or greater, it represents a Unix timestamp, and the transaction can only be included after the corresponding date and time has passed. Note that the time-based nLockTime is compared to the 11-block median time past (the median timestamp of the 11 blocks preceding the block in which the transaction is mined), and that median time past tends to lag the block time by about one hour (give or take).

The `and(POL_1,POL_2)` policy term specifies that both subpolicies, `POL_1` and `POL_2`, must be satisfied in order for the transaction to be valid. This means that whatever conditions or requirements are defined by `POL_1` and `POL_2` must both be met simultaneously.

The `hash160(HEX)` policy term specifies that the spender provides a piece of data (preimage) that, when processed through the SHA-256 hash function followed by the RIPEMD-160 hash function, results in the specified 40-character hexadecimal string represented by `HEX`. This ensures that the spender has knowledge of the original data that hashes to the given value.

The `hash256(HEX)` policy term specifies that a participant provides a piece of data (preimage) that, when processed through the SHA-256 hash function twice, results in the specified 64-character hexadecimal string represented by `HEX`. This ensures that the spender has knowledge of the original data that hashes to the given value.

The `older(NUM)` policy term specifies that the transaction can only be included in a block if at least `NUM` blocks have been mined since the transaction's input was last confirmed in a block. Assuming an average block time of 10 minutes, this means the transaction must wait approximately `NUM * 10` minutes before it becomes valid for inclusion in the blockchain.

The `or(N@POL_1,POL_2)` policy term specifies that to unlock the funds, either one of two subpolicies must be satisfied: `POL_1` or `POL_2`. The `N@` before `POL_1` indicates that `POL_1` is `N` times more likely to be chosen or satisfied compared to `POL_2` when constructing a transaction.

The `or(POL_1,N@POL_2)` policy term specifies that to unlock the funds, either one of two subpolicies must be satisfied: `POL_1` or `POL_2`. The `N@` before `POL_2` indicates that `POL_2` is `N` times more likely to be chosen or satisfied compared to `POL_1` when constructing a transaction.
The `or(POL_1,POL_2)` policy term specifies that to unlock the funds protected by this policy, either one of two subpolicies, `POL_1` or `POL_2`, must be satisfied.

The `pk(NAME)` policy term specifies that a transaction must be signed by the private key corresponding to the public key named `NAME` in order to be valid. `NAME` is a placeholder for any string up to 16 characters that represents a specific public key.

The `ripemd160(HEX)` policy term specifies that the spender provides a piece of data (preimage) that, when processed through the RIPEMD-160 hash function, results in the specified 40-character hexadecimal string represented by `HEX`. This ensures that the spender has knowledge of the original data that hashes to the given value.

The `sha256(HEX)` policy term specifies that a participant provides a piece of data (preimage) that, when processed through the SHA-256 hash function, results in the specific 64-character hexadecimal string represented by `HEX`. This ensures that the spender has knowledge of the original data that hashes to the given value.

The `thresh(NUM,POL_1,POL_2,...,POL_n)` policy term specifies that at least `NUM` of the specified subpolicies (`POL_1`, `POL_2`, ..., `POL_n`) are fulfilled in order for the transaction to be valid. Each subpolicy (`POL_1`, `POL_2`, ..., `POL_n`) represents a separate condition that can be met independently.
